summary: Iter 24 — RE-VERIFY of fixes from iter22/23. ALL CRITICAL ISSUES RESOLVED. /live-tv now RENDERS cleanly (no TDZ on `bump`); the LIVE TV / ON NOW branding text is present and the page mounts. Escape navigates to '/' from EVERY tested route: /live-tv, /sports, /settings, /search, /sources, /networks/netflix (plural), /watch-together. Escape on Home (/) does not throw. SideNav dwell PASSES exactly per spec: initial width 76px, quick LEFT→RIGHT in <250ms stays at 76px (NOT expanded), LEFT + 400ms wait → 203px (expanded). Single tap-Enter on a /sports FixtureCard fires onClick/play EXACTLY ONCE (count delta = 1). Long-press (hold-OK) could not be reliably reproduced via Playwright's synthetic dispatchEvent because React 17+ event delegation on the root does not always re-fire synthetic onKeyDown for manually-dispatched native KeyboardEvents — but code review confirms the implementation is correct: SportsGuide.jsx 968-985 increments pressRef.count on each keydown, sets data-long-pressed='true' and calls onRemind when count >= 6 and !fired; useSpatialFocus.js 590-594 checks data-long-pressed on keyup and skips target.click() when present, removing the attribute. No regressions found. Build is sideload-ready from a D-pad / BACK perspective.
critical_code_review_comments: LiveTV.jsx: `const [bump, setBump] = useState(0)` and `const rerender = useCallback(...)` are now hoisted to lines 157-158, BEFORE the `channels` useMemo's `bump` dep (line 229). TDZ regression from iter23 is fixed. Recommend adding an ESLint rule `no-use-before-define` for let/const so future re-orderings break the build instead of crashing at runtime.

useBackHandler.js: clean capture-phase keydown handler is wired into all 7 main routes (Network.jsx confirmed at line 23). Consider adding a contenteditable guard alongside the existing INPUT/TEXTAREA guard for Backspace robustness.

SportsGuide FixtureCard long-press: implementation is correct but the threshold of `count >= 6` keydowns is duration-agnostic — on devices/keyboards with different auto-repeat rates the timing window (~600ms target) may drift. Consider switching to a timestamp-based threshold (`Date.now() - p.firstDownTs >= 500`) for hardware-portable timing.
SideNav dwell of 300ms works correctly (quick LEFT-RIGHT roundtrip in <250ms does not expand, LEFT + 400ms does).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (8/8 routes pass Escape→/, LiveTV no longer crashes, SideNav dwell verified, single-tap fires once)

test_credentials: None. Tester profile is seeded into localStorage: onnowtv-profiles-v1 = [{id:'tester-auto', name:'Tester', isTester:true, ...}]; onnowtv-active-profile-v1 = 'tester-auto' (PLAIN STRING — not JSON-encoded). Note for future agents: KEY_ACTIVE stores the profile id as a raw string, not a JSON-encoded object.
seed_data_creation: localStorage Tester profile seeded prior to each route test. Active-profile id is a plain string in localStorage, not JSON.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iter22/23 BACK-key and LiveTV-TDZ blockers are RESOLVED. If the long-press hold-OK behaviour needs a more rigorous Playwright assertion, dispatchEvent is unreliable with React 17+ delegation — instead inject a real KeyboardEvent via `await page.evaluate` that synthesises sequential native events AND manually invokes the React-bound handler via the element's `__reactProps$...` fiber property. Easier: do a `pageevaluate(() => { const card = ...; const reactProps = Object.keys(card).find(k=>k.startsWith('__reactProps')); for (let i=0; i<8; i++) card[reactProps].onKeyDown({key:'Enter', currentTarget: card, preventDefault:()=>{}}); ... })`. Or just trust the code review for this one — both files are short and reviewed.
rca of the issue: Iter23's LiveTV TDZ root cause was: `channels` useMemo at line ~219 read `bump` from its dep array, but `const [bump, setBump] = useState(0)` was declared at line ~407. JS const/let TDZ threw ReferenceError on first render, error boundary intercepted, useBackHandler effect never mounted. Fix: hoisted `const [bump, setBump] = useState(0)` and `const rerender = useCallback(...)` to lines 157-158 (above the `channels` useMemo at line 229). Verified by code inspection (`grep -n 'bump|setBump|rerender' LiveTV.jsx`) and runtime: /live-tv now renders the hero + grid with no error boundary. Iter22's /networks/:slug BACK regression was due to useBackHandler not being wired into Network.jsx — now fixed (Network.jsx line 23 calls useBackHandler('/')).
